response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with genuine systems chops, but his professional C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list. His recent decade centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang web platforms, with no evidence of MQTT, message brokers in C++, industrial automation, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practice. The low-latency DLang work shows he can reason about concurrency and performance, but this role demands deep, current C++ and industrial-cybersecurity fluency that the resume does not demonstrate.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second
Long, versatile engineering career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with a strong testing and TDD culture
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing through workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: Minimal and dated professional C++ experience (~7 months in 2014-2015); current stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
No industrial automation, IoT, MQTT, or message-broker background relevant to the domain
No exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial-cybersecurity practices required by the role
